items_en: 1. Reaffirms the importance of the effective continuation of the activities of the United Nations Relief and Works Agency for Palestine Refugees in the Near East in all areas of operation;

2. Expresses its appreciation to the Commissioner-General and to all staff of the Agency for their tireless efforts and outstanding work, particularly in view of the difficult conditions, instability and crises faced over the past year;

3. Pays special tribute to the Agency for the essential role it has played since its inception more than 65 years ago in providing essential services to ensure the well-being, human development and protection of Palestine refugees and to improve their plight, as well as for the stability it has created

items_en: 4. Welcomes the Agency's extraordinary efforts, in cooperation with other United Nations agencies in the field, to provide emergency humanitarian assistance, including shelter and medical and food assistance to the refugees and civilians affected during military operations in the Gaza Strip in July and August 2014 and thereafter, as well as its exemplary capacity to mobilize in emergency situations while continuing to deliver its major human development programmes;
5. Welcomes the important support extended by the Governments of the host countries to the Agency in the performance of its tasks and their cooperation with the Agency;
6. Expresses its appreciation to the Advisory Commission of the Agency and requests it to continue its work and to keep it informed of its activities;

7. Takes note with appreciation of the report of the Working Group on the Financing of the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime 13 and the measures it is taking to help ensure its financial security, and requests the Secretary-General to provide the necessary services and assistance to the Working Group in the performance of its tasks;

8. Welcomes the Agency's six-year medium-term strategy for the period 2016-2021 and the continuing efforts of the Commissioner-General to increase the budgetary transparency and efficiency of the Agency, as reflected in his programme budget for the biennium 2016-2017; 14

9. Commends the Agency for continuing its reform efforts despite the difficult operational conditions, and takes note of the establishment of procedures to ensure maximum efficiency in order to reduce operating and administrative costs and optimize the use of resources;

10. Takes note of the update of the Special Report of the Commissioner-General of 3 August 2015 submitted pursuant to paragraph 21 of General Assembly resolution 302 (IV), transmitted on 15 September 2016 to the President of the General Assembly by the Secretary-General, on the serious financial crisis facing the Agency, and urges States and all international organizations to participate actively in the follow-up to the conclusions and recommendations contained therein;

11. Also takes note of the letter of 19 September 2016, in which the President of the General Assembly calls for a firm and collective commitment to remedy as soon as possible the precarious situation of the Agency, in particular through the implementation of measures in response to the recommendations contained in the update to the special report;

12. Welcomes the ambitious measures taken by the Office to address the recurrent financial crisis and urges it to continue its efforts to reduce the shortfalls in its programme budget so as to ensure the implementation of its main programmes;

items_en: 13. Invites all donors and parties concerned to support the Agency in order to ensure a stable and sustainable financial situation and to assist it in maintaining its main programmes pending the equitable settlement of the question of Palestine refugees;

14. Takes note with appreciation of the report of the Secretary-General on strengthening the management capacity of the Agency, and urges all Member States to give close consideration to its findings and recommendations, including the continuation of funding from the regular budget of the United Nations, taking into account the recurrent budget deficit of the Office;

15. Approves the efforts of the Commissioner-General to continue to provide all possible humanitarian assistance, as a temporary emergency measure, to persons in the region who are displaced and in great need of continued assistance as a result of recent crises in the Agency's areas of operations;

16. Encourages the Agency to strengthen its assistance to Palestine refugees in the Syrian Arab Republic who have suffered as a result of the crisis, as well as to those who have fled to neighbouring countries, in accordance with its mandate and regional plans to address the situation of Palestine refugees in the Syrian Arab Republic, and calls upon donors to ensure prompt and sustained support to the Agency in this regard, taking into account the serious deterioration of the situation and the growing needs of refugees;

17. Welcomes the progress made to date by the Agency in the reconstruction of the Nahr el-Bared refugee camp in northern Lebanon, and appeals to donors to ensure that the work can be completed as soon as possible, that relief to the internally displaced as a result of the destruction of that camp in 2007 is maintained and that, in order to alleviate the continuing suffering of those persons, the necessary support and financial assistance is provided until the reconstruction of the camp is completed;

18. Encourages the Agency, in close cooperation with other relevant United Nations bodies, to continue its efforts to address the needs, rights and protection of children, women and persons with disabilities in its activities, including through the provision of necessary psychosocial and humanitarian assistance, in accordance with the Convention on the Rights of the Child 16 , the Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination against Women 17 and the Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities 18;

19. Recognizes the serious protection needs of Palestine refugees in the region and supports the efforts of the Agency to contribute to a coordinated and sustained response to them in accordance with international law, including by strengthening its protection machinery and function in all its offices, including child protection;

Note  avec  satisfaction la  contribution  positive  des  programmes  de microfinancement et de crÃĐation d'emplois de l'Office, encourage les efforts visant Ã  accroÃŪtre la durabilitÃĐ des services de microfinancement et Ã  en faire bÃĐnÃĐficier un plus grand nombre de rÃĐfugiÃĐs de Palestine, compte tenu notamment de leurs taux de chÃīmage ÃĐlevÃĐs, en particulier chez les jeunes, se fÃĐlicite des mesures prises par l'Office pour rÃĐduire les coÃŧts et ÃĐlargir les services de microfinancement au moyen de rÃĐformes internes, et demande Ã  l'Office de continuer d'aider, en coopÃĐration ÃĐtroite  avec  les  organismes  compÃĐtents,  Ã   stabiliser  la  situation  ÃĐconomique  et sociale des rÃĐfugiÃĐs dans tous les secteurs d'activitÃĐ ;
29. Demande une nouvelle fois Ã  tous les Ãtats et Ã  toutes les institutions spÃĐcialisÃĐes  et  organisations  non  gouvernementales  de  maintenir  et  d'augmenter
items_en: 20. Commends the Agency for its provision of humanitarian and psychosocial assistance and other initiatives to provide children with recreational, cultural and educational activities wherever it operates, including in the Gaza Strip, and, recognizing the benefits of these initiatives, calls for their full support by donors and host countries, and encourages the establishment and strengthening of partnerships to facilitate and improve the delivery of these services;
21. Calls upon Israel, the occupying Power, to comply fully with the provisions of the Geneva Convention relative to the Protection of Civilian Persons in Time of War, of 12 August 1949; 6

22. Also calls upon Israel to comply with Articles 100, 104 and 105 of the Charter of the United Nations and the Convention on the Privileges and Immunities of the United Nations 4 in order to ensure in all circumstances the safety of the staff of the Agency, the protection of its institutions and the safety of its facilities in the Occupied Palestinian Territory, including East Jerusalem;

23. Takes note of the investigations into incidents affecting the premises of the Agency during the conflict in the Gaza Strip in July and August 2014, and requests that those responsible for all violations of international law be held accountable;

24. Urges the Government of Israel to promptly reimburse the Agency for all transit costs and other financial losses caused by delays and restrictions on the freedom of movement and access imposed by Israel;

25. Calls upon Israel, in particular, to cease obstructing the movement and access of Agency personnel, vehicles and supplies and to cease levying taxes, additional duties and charges, which are detrimental to the activities of the Agency;

26. Reiterates its call upon Israel to lift fully the restrictions on or delaying the importation of construction materials and supplies necessary for the reconstruction and repair of thousands of damaged or destroyed refugee shelters and for the implementation of outstanding civilian infrastructure projects that are sorely lacking in the refugee camps in the Gaza Strip, noting the alarming figures contained in the report of the United Nations country team entitled "Gaza: two years after" of 26 August 2016;
27. Requests the Commissioner-General to continue to issue identity cards to Palestine refugees and their descendants in the Occupied Palestinian Territory;

28. Notes with appreciation the positive contribution of the Agency's microfinance and job creation programmes, encourages efforts to increase the sustainability of microfinance services and to increase the number of Palestine refugees, including in the light of their high unemployment rates, in particular among young people, welcomes the measures taken by the Agency to reduce costs and expand microfinance services through internal reforms, and requests the Agency to continue to assist, in close cooperation with relevant agencies, in stabilizing the economic and social situation of refugees in all sectors of activity;
29. Reiterates its call upon all States, specialized agencies and non-governmental organizations to maintain and increase

text_fr: leurs contributions au financement du budget-programme de l'Office, d'accroÃŪtre les allocations de fonds qui servent Ã  financer les subventions et bourses d'enseignement  supÃĐrieur  devant  ÃŠtre  accordÃĐes  aux  rÃĐfugiÃĐs  de  Palestine,  et  de participer  Ã   la  crÃĐation  de  centres  de  formation  professionnelle  Ã   l'intention  des rÃĐfugiÃĐs, et prie l'Office d'encaisser et de gÃĐrer lesdites allocations ;
text_en: their contributions to the programme budget of the Agency, to increase the allocation of funds for higher education grants and scholarships to be granted to Palestine refugees, and to participate in the establishment of vocational training centres for refugees, and requests the Agency to collect and manage those allocations;

items_fr: 30. Demande instamment Ã  tous les Ãtats, aux institutions spÃĐcialisÃĐes et aux organisations  non  gouvernementales  de  soutenir  le  travail  ÃĐminemment  utile  et nÃĐcessaire que l'Office accomplit en faveur des rÃĐfugiÃĐs de Palestine dans toutes ses zones d'opÃĐrations en apportant des contributions Ã  cet organisme ou en augmentant leur montant afin de remÃĐdier aux graves difficultÃĐs financiÃĻres qu'il connaÃŪt et Ã  l'insuffisance  de  financement  de  son  budget-programme,  notant  que  ses  besoins financiers se sont encore accrus du fait des conflits et de l'instabilitÃĐ rÃĐcents et de la dÃĐtÃĐrioration de la situation humanitaire sur le terrain ;

31. Demande Ã  cet ÃĐgard aux donateurs d'assurer rapidement le financement intÃĐgral des programmes de secours d'urgence, de relÃĻvement et de reconstruction formulÃĐs dans le cadre de ses appels d'urgence et de ses plans d'intervention ;

32. Demande au SecrÃĐtaire gÃĐnÃĐral de faciliter la tenue de vastes consultations avec les Ãtats Membres, en particulier les pays hÃītes, les membres de la Commission consultative et les autres donateurs, ainsi qu'avec des institutions financiÃĻres internationales, afin d'ÃĐtudier tous les moyens ÃĐventuels, notamment les contributions  volontaires  et  quotes-parts,  susceptibles  de  garantir  Ã   l'Office  un financement suffisant, prÃĐvisible et soutenu tout au long de son mandat, et le prie de lui prÃĐsenter pour examen, d'ici Ã  mars 2017, les rÃĐsultats de ces consultations et les recommandations formulÃĐes Ã  son intention, sans prÃĐjudice de l'avis des organes compÃĐtents.
items_en: 30. Urges all States, the specialized agencies and non-governmental organizations to support the highly useful and necessary work of the Agency in favour of Palestine refugees in all its areas of operation by contributing to or increasing the level of contributions to the Agency in order to address the serious financial difficulties and lack of funding for its programme budget, noting that its financial requirements have increased further as a result of recent conflicts and instability and the deteriorating humanitarian situation on the ground;

31. Calls upon donors, in this regard, to ensure timely and full funding for emergency relief, rehabilitation and reconstruction programmes in the context of its emergency appeals and contingency plans;

32. Requests the Secretary-General to facilitate extensive consultations with Member States, in particular host countries, members of the Advisory Commission and other donors, as well as with international financial institutions, in order to explore all possible means, including voluntary contributions and assessed contributions, to ensure adequate, predictable and sustained funding for the Agency throughout its mandate, and requests the Secretary-General to submit to the General Assembly for its consideration, by March 2017, the results of those consultations and the recommendations made to the Assembly, without prejudice to the views of the relevant bodies.

text_en: 53rd plenary meeting 6 December 2016
